description:
The FRAC-17 fix (commit a9ede7c, "darker octahedron tint") is the wrong direction. User feedback:
- The tinted colors applied to face banner images are wrong (need to be respecified)
- One face is missing its photo entirely

## Current state (post-FRAC-17)
- src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx tints each banner with the section accent color: Visit #889460, Events #D4857A, Campus #2B5A48, Education #C41E20, Publications #E870A0
- Same nav-node accents are reused as the per-face tint multiplier instead of MeshBasicMaterial color=#fff

## What's wrong (needs human input)
- Which colors should each face actually be? Are the nav accents themselves wrong, or just the tint-as-image-multiplier approach?
- Which face is missing a photo, and what photo should it be?
- Should the tint be removed entirely (back to white/no tint), darkened differently, or replaced with per-face image colorways?

## Why needs_human
Cannot proceed without design intent — could revert FRAC-17 outright, but the user wants something different than either pre- or post-FRAC-17 state.

## Relationship to FRAC-19 (DESIGN.md)
The current octahedron colors should NOT be canonicalized into DESIGN.md until this is resolved. FRAC-19 will note current values descriptively but flag them as in-flux pending this task.
title: [Bug] Octahedron — wrong tint colors and missing face photo (FRAC-17 fix incorrect)
